title: 5/27/19 Tribes of America for Google Earth

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder. V-Log for May 27th, 2019 on Tuesday at 5.36 in Des Moines.
Yesterday I did a V-Log about this time of the day, and then I went to sleep, and then I got up a little bit before noon, actually.
So I only got like five hours of sleep or something, six hours.
And then, um, and then, uh, but I was, I wasn't tired or anything.
I went over to my mom's house and helped her just fill up the hole that I made yesterday from removing the tree and some stuff, organizing it.
And then she fed me some lunch, so that was cool.
And then I came back here and I was working on my dealio.
I'll show you.
But, uh, before yesterday, I only had a couple of those diggity done.
So, um, it was working on, and I think I just started today, or yesterday.
Um, and the ones that I did do already, I had to kind of remove them, so I don't know how I got them there and what, they didn't make any sense.
So I did them all from this one big map, uh, the map's too big to put it in one place across America.
So I had to just build lots of copies of it for different spots and traced in the, uh, kingdoms, the, the, the tribes.
And then, you know, I'm kind of closing them together.
So I'll finish that tomorrow and then I'll organize where they go and the colors they need to be.
And who knows, maybe even do the videos of them, Google Earth.
No, I don't think I'll do the tours of them, but, um, video tours.
But, uh, yeah, um, that was essential to do Tribes of America because I was going to attach it to my timeline afterwards.
Because I have all the, most of the other tribes done, um, I, I actually built a whole, but, uh, I've lost a lot of work that I've done over the years.
Um, uh, I, I built all the tribes from, um, Herodotus histories, like tribes that he describes in North Africa that I lost.
I built the entire water network of ancient China and Babylon that got lost.
Because back when I started doing this, I didn't realize some things, some problems that Google Earth has.
It likes to crash all the time.
And, um, you know, so of course I lost a bunch of stuff from it crashing because you don't always save it all the time.
And then, but then what happened was it brought it, it brought a copy back.
And then I saved that copy, which saved over the copy that I had saved.
And, and the copy that it brings back is old, like weeks old.
So I lost a few weeks of work.
That's how I lost those.
So, so I know now whenever Google Earth crashes and then I open it again, I have to make sure to immediately delete the copy that it brings up.
Cause it always brings up some other copy.
It's weird.
I don't know where it even comes from, but, but anyway.
Um, so yeah, doing, uh, bullet, uh, tribes and it was, it was interesting to build it, you know, and finally kind of know exactly where all those tribes are.
When you build this stuff and you take forever, it gets ingrained in your head, you know?

Um, so yeah, I'll do this and then I'll probably take the rest of the day and then, yeah, once I get done, um, I'll get back on the timeline and then I'll yell, I'll, I'll, I'll link more of the K of Google Earth files until I do all the ones that are already built.

And at that point I can, and as I move along, I can modify the actual timeline itself, you know, and do random things, you know, like the other day I was, began the, the, uh, a King's list of the kingdom of Kanoct, but the computer crashed.
Uh, Dreamweaver crashed like twice in like 20 minutes.
So I gave up on it.
But, um, uh, yeah, and hopefully I didn't lose other work.
Cause when it crashes, it, it only can recover the one that you're working on.
It can't recover all the pages.
So if you, if, if all the pages are unsaved, then you lose stuff in the other ones.
But I think that the other ones were saved.
So anyway, um, last night I had my second time.
I went to, I drove down to, um, I drove down to, uh, 7-11 cause last night, not last night, but night before last was the first time.
I went there and I found, cause I wanted a slushie, a slurpee cause I saw some video about 7-11 and they mentioned the slurpee.
And then I remembered the one here and the 24 hours.
I had never been there at night.
So then I came back tonight to get a bigger slurpee and then a hot dog.
But, uh, so I guess I'm not that hungry now for breakfast.
Actually, I just had a can of, of corn.
Um, so yeah, that's what I'm up to.
Um, I'm, I'm pleased that I busted through all the tribes in America in one day.
Um, that's encouraging.
I'm not so sure I could have done that a few years ago.
You know, I'm getting faster and better.
Um, as time goes on, I guess.
So, uh, yeah, I'm going to have me in my nap.
Then when I get up, I'm going to perfect these.
There's one that I need to perfect that I know now.
And yeah, and then organize them.
Cause the way that I built them, I just built them fast and I didn't bother about organizing
Them.
So that's what I'll do.
Um, and then yeah, collect, connect them, upload them, connect them to, uh, my timeline,
You know, and then the link, the entire, you know, like the entire folder of all the tribes
To my timeline, people can download.
Okay.
So that's enough talking.
Talk to you tomorrow.
